name: Anabasine
description: Anabasine is a minor tobacco alkaloid with nicotinic pharmacology and recognized use as an exposure biomarker. Evidence is strongest for toxicology and exposure characterization rather than therapeutic benefit.
effects: Human exposure studies use anabasine as a minor tobacco alkaloid biomarker.
Nicotine-like pharmacology implies stimulant and dependence-related risk context.
contraindications: Avoid use during pregnancy and in patients with unstable cardiovascular disease.
interactions: Potential additive sympathomimetic effects with nicotine or other stimulants.

sources.title: Assessment of the Exposure to Tobacco-Specific Nitrosamines and Minor Tobacco Alkaloids in Users of Various Tobacco/Nicotine Products.
